acceptance_criteria:
1. core/services.py ProjectService class exists and delegates to SQL repos.
2. All 17 PM agent tools use ProjectService instead of direct repo instantiation.
3. Reporter context.py loads data via ProjectService.
4. Agent entry points use create_tables() instead of repo initialise() calls.
5. create_demo_data.py seeds SQL database via core.db.seed.
6. reset.py drops/recreates SQL tables instead of deleting JSON files.
7. Old project_manager_agent/ directory and JSON data files are removed.
8. Agent daily loop runs end-to-end against SQL with no behaviour change.
9. CLAUDE.md updated with new architecture and commands.
description: Rewire both the Project Manager agent and Reporter agent to read/write data via the new SQLite-backed service layer (ProjectService) instead of directly instantiating JSON file repositories. Create a thin service facade in core/services.py. Update create_demo_data.py and reset.py to work with SQL. Delete old JSON repositories and data files.
title: Epic 2: Migrate Agent Toolset to SQL

acceptance_criteria:
1. core/services.py exists with ProjectService class.
2. Constructor accepts optional Session, creates repos internally.
3. Task methods: list_tasks() -> list[Task], update_task_status(), update_task_blocking().
4. Project methods: get_project() -> Project, update_project_health(), update_milestone().
5. RAID methods: list_raid_items() -> list[RaidItem], add_raid_item(), update_raid_item().
6. Action methods: list_actions() -> list[Action], add_action(), update_action_status().
7. Message methods: send_message(), list_inbox(), list_outbox().
8. Journal methods: read_last_journal(), write_journal(), list_journals().
9. Reports: list_reports() reads data/reports/ directory.
10. close() properly closes the session.
description: Create core/services.py with a concrete ProjectService class that acts as a thin facade over all repositories. It owns the DB session lifecycle and instantiates all SQL repos + FileJournalRepository in its constructor. Methods are 1-3 line delegations to repos. Includes list_reports() for reading report files from data/reports/. Provides close() to clean up the session.
title: Create ProjectService facade in core/services.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. No imports from repositories.py — all imports from core.services.
2. All 17 tool functions use ProjectService instead of direct repos.
3. fetch_tasks_tool lambda rewired to use service.
4. fetch_project_plan returns serialised Project dataclass.
5. fetch_raid_items/fetch_actions return serialised typed objects.
6. add_raid_item/add_action construct domain objects before passing to service.
7. Tool names, descriptions, and Pydantic input schemas are unchanged.
8. All tools still return strings/dicts that the LLM can interpret.
description: Update agents/project_manager/tools.py to replace all direct repo instantiation (TasksRepo(), ProjectRepo(), RaidRepo(), ActionsRepo(), Mailbox(), Journal()) with ProjectService calls. Each tool function creates a ProjectService instance, calls the appropriate method, and closes it. Adapt tools that return dicts to work with typed domain objects: fetch_project_plan returns Project (serialise via asdict for LLM), fetch_raid_items returns list[RaidItem] (serialise via asdict), add_raid_item constructs RaidItem, add_action constructs Action. Tool names, Pydantic schemas, and descriptions remain identical — no LLM prompt changes.
title: Rewire PM agent tools to use ProjectService

acceptance_criteria:
1. No imports from repositories.py — all from core.services.
2. load_all() uses ProjectService for all data fetching.
3. Filtering logic works with typed objects (attribute access, not dict access).
4. format_context() outputs the same prompt string format as before.
5. Reporter generates a valid status report when run end-to-end.
description: Update agents/reporter/context.py to replace direct repo instantiation with ProjectService calls. Adapt load_all() to work with typed dataclasses: tasks as list[Task] (use t.status instead of t['status']), project as Project (use project.rag_status instead of project['rag_status']), RAID items as list[RaidItem], actions as list[Action]. Update format_context() similarly.
title: Rewire reporter context.py to use ProjectService

acceptance_criteria:
1. Agent entry point calls create_tables() instead of individual repo initialise().
2. Journal directory is still created if missing.
3. Agent starts and runs daily loop without errors.
4. No imports from old repositories.py.
description: Update agents/project_manager/agent.py __main__ block to replace repo initialise() calls (TasksRepo().initialise(), ProjectRepo().initialise(), etc.) with create_tables() from core.db.engine and FileJournalRepository directory initialisation. The graph construction and LLM configuration remain unchanged.
title: Rewire PM agent entry point for SQL initialisation

acceptance_criteria:
1. create_demo_data.py imports from core.db.engine and core.db.seed.
2. Running the script creates data/project_manager.db with all demo data.
3. Journal files and date.json are still created.
4. No JSON data files are written (no tasks.json, project.json, etc.).
description: Update the root-level create_demo_data.py to call create_tables() then seed_demo_data(session) from core.db.seed instead of writing JSON files directly. The script should also seed journal files and set the reference date.
title: Update create_demo_data.py to seed SQL database

acceptance_criteria:
1. reset.py drops and recreates all SQL tables.
2. Journal files in data/journal/ are deleted.
3. --reports flag still deletes data/reports/ contents.
4. --date flag still resets data/date.json.
5. No references to JSON data files.
description: Update root-level reset.py to drop all SQL tables (Base.metadata.drop_all) and recreate them (create_tables()) instead of deleting JSON files. Keep existing behaviour: delete journal markdown files, optionally delete reports (--reports flag), reset data/date.json.
title: Update reset.py for SQL database reset

acceptance_criteria:
1. Old project_manager_agent/ directory at root is deleted.
2. JSON data files (tasks.json, project.json, raid.json, actions.json) are deleted.
3. data/inbox/ and data/outbox/ directories are deleted.
4. .gitignore includes data/project_manager.db.
5. No import errors — all code references the src/ layout.
description: Delete the old project_manager_agent/ directory (at project root, not under src/) and all JSON data files that have been replaced by SQL: data/tasks.json, data/project.json, data/raid.json, data/actions.json, data/inbox/, data/outbox/. Update .gitignore to include data/project_manager.db.
title: Remove old project_manager_agent/ directory and JSON data files

acceptance_criteria:
1. CLAUDE.md reflects src/ layout and core/agents/web structure.
2. Run commands use new module paths.
3. Architecture section describes DDD layers: domain models, protocols, ORM, services.
4. Data layer section describes SQLite + file-based journal/reports.
5. No references to old JSON repositories or flat file storage.
description: Update CLAUDE.md to document the new src/ layout, three-package architecture (core/agents/web), SQLite storage, run commands (new module paths), and the service layer pattern. Remove references to old JSON file structure and old module paths.
title: Update CLAUDE.md with new architecture documentation

acceptance_criteria:
1. tests/test_services.py tests ProjectService methods.
2. Tests use in-memory SQLite for isolation.
3. Agent daily loop completes without errors against seeded SQL data.
4. Reporter generates a valid markdown report from SQL data.
5. All tests pass with uv run pytest tests/.
description: Create tests/test_services.py with unit tests for ProjectService methods against in-memory SQLite. Also create an end-to-end smoke test that seeds the demo data, runs the PM agent daily loop, and verifies the agent completes without errors. Run the reporter agent to verify report generation from SQL.
title: Write service layer tests and end-to-end agent smoke test

acceptance_criteria:
1. FastAPI app serves at localhost:8000 with working routes for dashboard, tasks, RAID, journal, and reports.
2. Dashboard shows project summary, RAG status, milestone table, and task counts.
3. Tasks page shows all tasks with status filtering via AlpineJS.
4. RAID page shows RAID log with type-tab filtering (risks/assumptions/issues/decisions).
5. Journal page lists dates and renders markdown entries.
6. Reports page lists dates and renders markdown reports.
7. Web package imports only from core/, never from agents/.
8. All web route tests pass via FastAPI TestClient.
9. No build tools or npm required — AlpineJS and Pico CSS loaded from CDN.
description: Build a read-only web dashboard using FastAPI with Jinja2 templates and AlpineJS for client-side interactivity. The web layer reads from the SQLite database via the shared ProjectService, providing views for project status, tasks, RAID log, journals, and reports. The web package depends only on core/ — never on agents/.
title: Epic 3: Web UI Dashboard (FastAPI + AlpineJS)

acceptance_criteria:
1. fastapi, uvicorn[standard], jinja2, markdown in pyproject.toml dependencies.
2. uv sync installs without errors.
3. python -c 'import fastapi; import jinja2; import markdown' succeeds.
description: Update pyproject.toml to add web UI dependencies: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], jinja2, markdown. Run uv sync to install.
title: Add FastAPI, Uvicorn, Jinja2, and Markdown dependencies

acceptance_criteria:
1. web/app.py creates a FastAPI app with title 'Project Manager Dashboard'.
2. Static files mounted at /static.
3. Jinja2Templates configured for web/templates/.
4. get_service() dependency yields ProjectService and closes on exit.
5. All 5 routers included.
6. App starts with uvicorn without errors (even if templates don't exist yet).
description: Create src/project_manager_agent/web/app.py with FastAPI application factory. Mount static files from web/static/. Configure Jinja2 template directory. Create get_service() dependency that yields a ProjectService instance and closes it after the request. Include all route modules (dashboard, tasks, raid, journal, reports).
title: Create FastAPI app factory in web/app.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. Five route modules exist in web/routes/.
2. GET / returns dashboard with project summary, RAG status, milestone table, task counts.
3. GET /tasks returns all tasks with status, owner, due date, dependencies.
4. GET /raid returns RAID log with all item types.
5. GET /journal lists dates; GET /journal/{date} renders markdown content.
6. GET /reports lists dates; GET /reports/{date} renders markdown content.
7. All routes use get_service() dependency — no direct repo instantiation.
8. All routes return HTML responses.
description: Create 5 route modules in web/routes/: dashboard.py (GET / - project summary, RAG, milestones, task counts), tasks.py (GET /tasks - all tasks with status info), raid.py (GET /raid - RAID log entries), journal.py (GET /journal - date list, GET /journal/{date} - rendered markdown), reports.py (GET /reports - date list, GET /reports/{date} - rendered markdown). All routes are read-only, server-rendered via Jinja2. Use ProjectService via dependency injection.
title: Create web route modules for all dashboard views

acceptance_criteria:
1. base.html has nav sidebar with links to all 5 sections.
2. AlpineJS loaded from CDN in base.html.
3. Pico CSS loaded from CDN for classless baseline styling.
4. tasks.html has AlpineJS filter controls for task status.
5. raid.html has AlpineJS tab switching for RAID types.
6. journal.html and reports.html render markdown as HTML.
7. style.css exists with custom styling.
8. No npm, no build step required.

Create web/static/style.css for custom styles. No build tools, no npm — all client dependencies from CDN.

acceptance_criteria:
1. tests/test_web.py exists with tests for all 5 route groups.
2. Tests seed an in-memory SQLite DB with demo data.
3. GET / returns 200 with project name in response.
4. GET /tasks returns 200 with task descriptions.
5. GET /raid returns 200 with RAID item titles.
6. GET /journal returns 200 with date listing.
7. GET /reports returns 200 with report listing.
8. All tests pass with uv run pytest tests/test_web.py.

Seed the database with demo data, then assert each route returns 200 and contains expected key content in the HTML response (e.g. project name on dashboard, task descriptions on tasks page, RAID titles on RAID page).
title: Write web route tests with FastAPI TestClient

acceptance_criteria:
1. Outbox messages are tagged with task_id; chaser frequency check is per-task, not per-person.
2. Escalation messages sent to a configurable recipient after N days overdue with no response.
3. Inbox messages are parsed for intent: completion confirmation, blocker report, date change request.
4. Agent decisions are driven by parsed intent rather than treating all replies as raw text.
5. Message model in SQL includes task_id field.
6. Escalation threshold N is configurable.
description: Make the agent's communication smarter: track outbox messages per-task (not per-person), add escalation to managers for persistently overdue tasks, and parse inbox replies for structured intent (completion, blocker, date change). Should be implemented after Epic 4 as escalation builds on overdue detection and priority.
title: Epic 5: Communications Enhancement

acceptance_criteria:
1. Message dataclass and ORM model include optional task_id field.
2. send_message tool accepts optional task_id parameter.
3. Agent tags reminders with the relevant task_id when sending.
4. Chaser deduplication checks last message per (owner, task_id) pair, not just per owner.
5. Existing messages without task_id continue to work (field is nullable).
description: Add a task_id field to the Message model and SQL schema. When the agent sends a reminder for a specific task, tag the message with that task's ID. Update the chaser frequency check to be per-task rather than per-person — currently 'last message sent to Bob' is too coarse when Bob owns multiple tasks. Backlog item #4.
title: Tag outbox messages with task_id for per-task chaser tracking

acceptance_criteria:
1. Escalation triggers after N days overdue with no completion (default N=3).
2. Escalation message sent to a configurable escalation recipient.
3. Message references specific task, owner, days overdue.
4. Escalation is logged in the journal.
5. Re-escalation only occurs if overdue period extends significantly (e.g. doubles).
6. Escalation threshold N is configurable.
description: If a task is N days overdue with no completion or response, send an escalation message to a separate recipient (e.g. project sponsor or manager). N should be configurable (default: 3 days). Escalation should reference the task, owner, and how many days overdue. Only escalate once per task unless the situation worsens. Backlog item #8.
title: Add escalation logic for persistently overdue tasks

acceptance_criteria:
1. Inbox messages are parsed into intent categories: completion, blocker, date_change, general.
2. Completion intent triggers automatic task status update to complete.
3. Blocker intent triggers task blocking and optionally creates a RAID issue.
4. Date change intent is logged for PM review (or auto-updates if confidence is high).
5. Parsed intent is logged in the journal alongside the raw message.
6. Parsing uses the LLM — no brittle regex matching.
description: Extract structured intent from inbox messages using LLM parsing: completion confirmation, blocker report, date change request, general update, etc. Use the parsed intent to drive agent decisions rather than treating all replies as raw text. For example, a completion confirmation should trigger task status update; a blocker report should trigger task blocking and RAID item creation. Backlog item #9.
title: Parse inbox messages for structured intent

acceptance_criteria:
1. All structured data (tasks, project, phases, milestones, RAID items, actions, messages) is stored in SQLite.
2. Domain dataclasses remain pure Python with no SQLAlchemy dependency.
3. ORM models in core/db/orm.py map to SQL tables and repos convert between ORM and domain.
4. All repository protocols are defined and all SQL repos conform to them, returning typed domain objects.
5. src/ layout is in place: core/, agents/, web/ as peer packages.
6. All existing tests pass and new repo tests pass against in-memory SQLite.
7. pyproject.toml uses src/ layout and includes sqlalchemy>=2.0.
description: Migrate the project from flat JSON/JSONL file storage to a SQLite database using SQLAlchemy 2.0, while restructuring the codebase into a src/ layout with core/, agents/, and web/ as peer packages. Establish domain models (DDD), repository protocols (Protocol classes), and SQLite-backed repository implementations. Journal and reports stay as files; tasks, project, RAID, actions, and messages move to SQL.
title: Epic 1: SQLite Data Layer + src/ Restructure

acceptance_criteria:
1. src/project_manager_agent/ exists with core/, agents/, web/ subdirectories.
2. All existing code moved to correct locations under src/.
3. pyproject.toml configured for src/ layout.
4. All imports updated and no import errors when running the agent.
5. uv run python -m project_manager_agent.agents.project_manager.agent starts without import errors.
description: Create the src/project_manager_agent/ directory structure with core/, agents/project_manager/, agents/reporter/ as peer packages. Move existing code into the new structure. Update pyproject.toml to use src/ layout with [tool.setuptools.packages.find] where = ["src"]. Update all internal imports to use new paths (e.g. from project_manager_agent.core.models import Task). Ensure the project still runs after the restructure.
title: Restructure project to src/ layout

acceptance_criteria:
1. core/models.py contains all domain dataclasses: Task, Phase, Milestone, Project, RaidItem, Action, Message.
2. Project.phases is list[Phase], Project.milestones is list[Milestone], Project.objectives is list[str].
3. Message dataclass has direction Literal['inbound','outbound'] and all required fields.
4. No SQLAlchemy imports in this file — pure Python dataclasses only.
5. JsonSerialiser present and handles dt.date serialisation.
description: Move project_manager_agent/models.py to src/project_manager_agent/core/models.py. Tighten loose type hints: Project.phases to list[Phase], Project.milestones to list[Milestone], Project.objectives to list[str]. Add a new Message dataclass for inbox/outbox records with fields: message_id, direction (inbound/outbound), timestamp, owner_name, owner_email, message, sender_name, sender_email. Keep JsonSerialiser for journal writes.
title: Move and tighten domain models in core/models.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. core/protocols.py exists with 6 Protocol classes.
2. TaskRepository: list_all() -> list[Task], get(), update_status(), update_blocking().
3. ProjectRepository: read() -> Project, update_health(), update_milestone().
4. RaidRepository: list_all() -> list[RaidItem], add(item: RaidItem) -> int, update().
5. ActionRepository: list_all() -> list[Action], add(action: Action) -> int, update_status().
6. MessageRepository: send(), list_inbox() -> list[Message], list_outbox() -> list[Message].
7. JournalRepository: read_last(), write(), list_all().
8. No concrete implementations in this file.
description: Create core/protocols.py with typing.Protocol classes for all repositories. Each protocol defines the public API contract that SQL (and potentially other) implementations must satisfy. All methods return typed domain objects, not raw dicts. Protocols: TaskRepository, ProjectRepository, RaidRepository, ActionRepository, MessageRepository, JournalRepository.
title: Define repository protocol interfaces in core/protocols.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. core/db/orm.py defines Base and 7 ORM model classes.
2. TaskORM: task_id PK, depends_on as JSON text, all fields from Task dataclass.
3. ProjectORM: single row, objectives as JSON text.
4. PhaseORM: FK to ProjectORM.
5. MilestoneORM: FK to ProjectORM, linked_task_ids as JSON text.
6. RaidItemORM: all type-specific nullable columns present.
7. ActionORM: source_raid_id and source_task_id nullable.
8. MessageORM: direction column, autoincrement PK, sender fields nullable.
9. No domain model imports — ORM is independent of domain layer.
description: Create core/db/orm.py with SQLAlchemy 2.0 declarative ORM models mapping to SQL tables. Tables: tasks, projects (single row), phases (FK to projects), milestones (FK to projects), raid_items (flat with type-specific nullable columns), actions, messages (replaces inbox+outbox JSONL). Use JSON type or Text for list fields (depends_on, linked_task_ids, objectives). Define a shared Base = declarative_base().
title: Create SQLAlchemy ORM table models in core/db/orm.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. core/db/engine.py exists with engine, SessionLocal, get_session(), create_tables().
2. get_session() yields a Session and closes it on exit.
3. create_tables() creates all tables idempotently.
4. Running create_tables() twice does not error.
5. Database file created at data/project_manager.db when engine is used.
description: Create core/db/engine.py with SQLAlchemy engine creation, sessionmaker, a get_session() context manager that yields sessions, and a create_tables() function that calls Base.metadata.create_all(). DATABASE_URL defaults to sqlite:///data/project_manager.db. create_tables() must be idempotent (safe to call repeatedly).
title: Create database engine and session factory in core/db/engine.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. 5 SQL repo classes + 1 file-based repo class, all conforming to their Protocol.
2. Each SQL repo accepts Session, queries ORM models, returns domain dataclasses.
3. SqliteTaskRepository.list_all() returns list[Task] with proper date deserialisation.
4. SqliteProjectRepository.read() returns Project with list[Phase] and list[Milestone].
5. SqliteRaidRepository.list_all() returns list[RaidItem].
6. SqliteActionRepository.list_all() returns list[Action].
7. SqliteMessageRepository replaces Mailbox: send() inserts outbound, list_inbox()/list_outbox() filter by direction.
8. FileJournalRepository wraps existing file logic with configurable journal_dir.
9. All repos commit immediately on writes.
description: Create core/db/repositories.py with concrete repository implementations that satisfy the Protocol interfaces. Classes: SqliteTaskRepository, SqliteProjectRepository, SqliteRaidRepository, SqliteActionRepository, SqliteMessageRepository — each accepts a Session in constructor. Also FileJournalRepository that wraps existing file-based journal logic behind JournalRepository protocol, accepting journal_dir: Path as constructor arg. Each SQL repo maps between ORM models and domain dataclasses via _to_domain() helpers. Commits within each write method (immediate persistence).
title: Implement SQLite-backed repositories in core/db/repositories.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. core/db/seed.py exists with seed_demo_data(session) function.
2. Calling seed_demo_data inserts the full demo scenario into SQL tables.
3. All 11 tasks, 3 phases, milestones, RAID items, actions, and messages present after seeding.
4. Data matches the existing create_demo_data.py output.
description: Create core/db/seed.py with a seed_demo_data(session: Session) function that inserts the Customer Portal Modernisation demo scenario via ORM objects. Port the logic from the current create_demo_data.py (11 tasks, project with 3 phases and milestones, 7 RAID items, 3 actions, inbox messages). Also seed journal markdown files for the demo dates.
title: Create demo data seeding in core/db/seed.py

acceptance_criteria:
1. pyproject.toml has src/ layout configuration.
2. sqlalchemy>=2.0 in dependencies.
3. pytest in dev dependencies.
4. uv sync installs all dependencies without errors.
description: Update pyproject.toml to: (1) configure src/ layout with [tool.setuptools.packages.find] where = ['src'], (2) add sqlalchemy>=2.0 to dependencies, (3) add pytest to dev dependencies. Ensure uv lock and uv sync work correctly.
title: Update pyproject.toml for src/ layout and SQLAlchemy dependency

acceptance_criteria:
1. tests/test_repositories.py exists with tests for all 6 repo classes.
2. Tests use in-memory SQLite (no file I/O for SQL repos).
3. Each repo has tests for: read/list, create/add, update, error cases.
4. FileJournalRepository tested with tmp_path fixture.
5. All tests pass with uv run pytest tests/test_repositories.py.
description: Create tests/test_repositories.py with unit tests for all SQL repository implementations using sqlite:///:memory: for isolation. Test pattern: create tables, seed known data, assert reads return expected domain objects, assert writes persist correctly, assert error cases (e.g. update nonexistent task raises ValueError). Also test FileJournalRepository against a temporary directory.
title: Write repository unit tests against in-memory SQLite

description: Transform the application from single-project to multi-project. Every data entity gains a project_id. The web app provides a project switcher and cross-project portfolio views. The PM agent runs scoped to one project at a time. This is prioritised first because building new features on a single-project architecture and then retrofitting multi-project is more costly than doing it upfront. See docs/plans/epic-5-multi-project.md for full design.
title: Epic 8: Multi-Project Support

description: Add project_id FK to tasks, raid_items, actions, messages tables. Write an Alembic or manual migration script. Backfill existing data with a default project ID. Add indexes on project_id columns.
title: Database schema migration

description: Update all repository classes to accept and filter by project_id. Update ProjectService constructor to take project_id. Update FileJournalRepository to use journal/{project_id}/ paths. Update report paths to reports/{project_id}/.
title: Repository and service layer scoping

description: PM agent accepts --project CLI argument. All tools operate within the scoped project_id. Agent prompt includes project name. Runner function takes project_id parameter. Reporter agent also scoped by project.
title: Agent multi-project scoping

description: Add project switcher UI component (dropdown in nav). Restructure routes to /projects/{project_id}/... pattern. Session/cookie stores selected project. Navigation updates to include project context.
title: Web app project switcher and routing

description: New landing page at / showing all projects in a card grid: project name, RAG status, percent tasks complete, next milestone, forecast end date. Click through to project-scoped views.
title: Portfolio dashboard

description: Web UI and API endpoints to create, edit, and archive projects. POST /api/projects, PUT /api/projects/{id}, DELETE /api/projects/{id} (soft delete/archive).
title: Project CRUD

description: Update create_demo_data.py and seed.py to create 2-3 demo projects with distinct data sets. Update reset.py to handle multi-project reset.
title: Demo data seeding for multiple projects

description: Redesign inbox/outbox views as a modern email client with a selectable list pane and reading pane. Built with TailwindCSS/TailwindUI. Two-column layout: message list (left, 1/3) and reading pane (right, 2/3). AlpineJS for interaction, tabs for inbox/outbox switching.
title: Epic 14: Email-Client Inbox & Outbox UI

description: GET /api/messages with query params: direction (inbound/outbound), page, per_page. Returns id, sender/recipient, subject snippet (first 80 chars of body), date, read status. Ordered by date descending. Acceptance: API returns paginated, filtered message list as JSON.
title: 14.1 — Message list API

description: Two-column TailwindCSS layout: left pane (~1/3 width) with stacked message list, right pane (~2/3) for reading. Message list items show sender name, subject/snippet, date, unread indicator. Selected message highlighted. Tabs for Inbox/Outbox switching. AlpineJS for selection state and tab switching. Acceptance: Can see message list; can switch between inbox and outbox; selected item is highlighted.
title: 14.2 — Email-client layout and message list pane

description: Right pane displays full message content for selected message. Header: from, to, date, related task (as link). Body: full message text with whitespace preserved. Empty state when no message selected. Auto-selects first message on page load. Acceptance: Clicking a message in the list shows its full content in the reading pane.
title: 14.3 — Reading pane

description: Add read boolean column to messages table. Mark as read when selected in reading pane (PATCH /api/messages/{id}/read). Unread messages display with bold text and/or blue dot indicator. Unread count shown on Inbox/Outbox tabs. Acceptance: New messages appear unread; selecting marks as read; visual distinction works.
title: 14.4 — Read/unread tracking

description: On small screens, list takes full width. Tapping a message navigates to a detail view (reading pane full-screen). Back button returns to list. Acceptance: Usable on mobile-width screens.
title: 14.5 — Responsive / mobile layout

description: Add action buttons to outbound emails that link back to a lightweight web interface. Task owners can respond with a single click (mark complete, report blocker, new forecast date, acknowledge). Uses HMAC-signed tokens for secure action links.
title: Epic 9: Actionable Email Buttons

description: Generate HMAC-signed tokens encoding (action_type, task_id, owner_email, expiry). Validate on receipt. Reject expired or tampered tokens. Acceptance: Tokens can be generated, validated, and expired tokens are rejected.
title: 9.1 — Action token generation and validation

description: FastAPI routes for /actions/{token}. GET renders the appropriate page (one-click confirmation or form). POST executes the action. Includes thank-you and expired-token screens. Acceptance: All action types render correct pages and execute correctly.
title: 9.2 — Action web endpoints

description: Jinja2 HTML+text email templates that include context-appropriate action buttons. Template selection based on message type (chaser, escalation, general reminder). Acceptance: Emails render with correct action buttons for each message type.
title: 9.3 — Email template rendering

description: Abstract sender with implementations for console (dev/logging), SMTP, and HTTP API (Mailgun/SendGrid). Configured via data/config.json. Acceptance: Can switch between sender implementations via config.
title: 9.4 — Pluggable email sender

description: When send_message_to_task_owner is called, render the email template with action buttons and dispatch via the configured sender. Store the rendered email reference in the messages table. Acceptance: Agent send_message triggers full email pipeline end-to-end.
title: 9.5 — Wire send_message to email pipeline

acceptance_criteria:
1. Weekly summary report generated at data/reports/YYYY-Www.md covering the week's journals.
2. Status snapshot written to data/status.json on each run with current task states.
3. Both artefacts are generated automatically as part of the agent daily loop or via standalone commands.
description: Add reporting capabilities beyond the daily status report: weekly summary across journals (tasks completed, overdue, at risk) and a machine-readable status snapshot for external tools. Note: the status snapshot (#11) is partially superseded by the web UI (Epic 3) but remains useful for CI/automation consumers.
title: Epic 6: Observability & Reporting

acceptance_criteria:
1. Weekly report generated at data/reports/YYYY-Www.md.
2. Report covers tasks completed, overdue, at risk during the week.
3. Key decisions and blockers summarised.
4. Can be run via standalone CLI command.
5. Report format is consistent with daily status reports.
description: Generate a weekly markdown summary covering all journals from the past 7 days: tasks completed, tasks now overdue, tasks at risk, key decisions made, and open blockers. Output to data/reports/YYYY-Www.md. Can run as a standalone command or be triggered at the end of a week's final agent run. Backlog item #10.
title: Generate weekly summary report across journals

acceptance_criteria:
1. data/status.json written on each agent run.
2. Contains: all task statuses, project RAG, milestone statuses, overdue count, blocked count.
3. File is valid JSON and parseable by external tools.
4. Snapshot reflects state after the agent's daily loop completes.
description: Write a data/status.json file on each agent run containing a snapshot of all task states, project health, and key metrics. Enables external tools, CI pipelines, and dashboards to consume current state without parsing journals or querying the DB directly. Partially superseded by the web UI (Epic 3) but remains useful for automation consumers. Backlog item #11.
title: Write machine-readable status snapshot on each agent run

close_reason: All templates migrated to Tailwind CSS. 19/19 tests passing.
description: Replace Pico CSS with Tailwind CSS (CDN) and redesign all templates with TailwindUI-inspired patterns for a more professional look. Includes responsive sidebar, improved typography, badge system, empty states, and consistent design language.
title: Migrate web UI from Pico CSS to Tailwind CSS

acceptance_criteria:
1. Config file (data/config.json or similar) controls chaser frequency, advance warning days, and escalation threshold.
2. Agent reads config at startup; no code changes needed to tune behaviour.
3. Idempotency guard prevents duplicate journal entries and message sends on same-day reruns.
4. Agent can safely be run multiple times per day without side effects.
description: Operational hardening: externalise hardcoded thresholds (chaser frequency, advance warning days, escalation threshold) into a config file, and add idempotency guards to prevent double-sends and duplicate journal entries if the agent runs more than once per day.
title: Epic 7: Configuration & Robustness

acceptance_criteria:
1. Config file exists at a known path (e.g. data/config.json).
2. Contains: chaser_frequency_days, advance_warning_days, escalation_threshold_days, escalation_recipient.
3. Agent loads config at startup and uses values throughout the daily loop.
4. Sensible defaults if config file is missing.
5. No code changes needed to tune thresholds.
description: Move hardcoded behaviour thresholds into a data/config.json (or similar) file: chaser frequency (how often to re-remind), advance warning days, escalation threshold (days overdue before escalating), escalation recipient. Agent reads config at startup. Changing behaviour should require only editing the config file, not code. Backlog item #12.
title: Externalise thresholds into a config file

acceptance_criteria:
1. Agent detects if today's daily loop has already completed.
2. On rerun, duplicate messages are not sent.
3. On rerun, duplicate journal sections are not appended.
4. Detection mechanism is reliable (e.g. completion marker in DB or journal).
5. Agent can be forced to rerun if needed (e.g. --force flag).
description: Prevent double-sends and duplicate journal entries if the agent runs more than once in a day (e.g. after a crash and restart). Before acting, check whether today's loop has already completed by looking for an existing journal entry or a run-completion marker. If already run, skip or warn rather than duplicating messages and entries. Backlog item #13.
title: Add idempotency guard for same-day agent reruns

description: Replace plain-markdown report view with a structured visual dashboard using cards, charts (Chart.js), and tables. Reporter agent outputs structured JSON alongside markdown. Web UI renders from JSON with fallback to markdown.
title: Epic 12: Visual Report Dashboard

description: Define a Pydantic model for report output: executive_summary, rag_status, milestone_statuses, task_statistics (by status, by phase), overdue_tasks, raid_highlights, recommendations. The reporter agent outputs this as JSON alongside the markdown. Acceptance: Schema defined and validated.
title: 12.1 — Structured report schema

description: Modify the reporter agent to produce structured JSON output (using the schema) in addition to the markdown file. Store as data/reports/YYYY-MM-DD.json alongside the .md. Acceptance: Reporter generates both .md and .json files.
title: 12.2 — Reporter agent structured output

description: GET /api/reports/{date} returns the structured JSON for a given report date. GET /api/reports returns list of available report dates. Acceptance: API serves structured report data.
title: 12.3 — Report data API endpoint

description: New report detail template with cards, tables, and Chart.js charts rendering the structured data. Tabbed view: Visual (default) and Markdown (raw). Responsive layout. Acceptance: Reports render as visual dashboard with charts and cards.
title: 12.4 — Visual report dashboard

description: Load data from multiple report JSONs to render trend lines: % tasks complete over time, open risk count, overdue task count. Displayed on the report page or as a dedicated /trends view. Acceptance: Trend charts show data across multiple report dates.
title: 12.5 — Trend charts across reports

description: Add ability to create inbound inbox messages from the web UI. Form-based composer that inserts rows into the messages table with direction=inbound. Allows stakeholders to send messages to the PM agent, simulating emails or chat messages.
title: Epic 13: Web Inbox Composer

description: Web form at /inbox/compose with fields: from, subject, body, related task (optional), date. Dropdown for 'from' populated from known stakeholders (task owners from DB). Date defaults to current REFERENCE_DATE. Form validation (from + body required). Acceptance: Can compose and submit a message; it appears in the inbox; PM agent picks it up on next run.
title: 13.1 — Inbox message composer form

description: Template selector dropdown at top of composer. Selecting a template pre-fills subject and body with placeholder text. Templates: Status Update, Blocker Report, Risk Escalation, Completion Notice, Question, Custom. Acceptance: Selecting a template fills the form; user can edit before sending.
title: 13.2 — Message templates

description: 'Send message about this task' link on task detail/list views. Opens composer with task pre-selected and subject pre-filled. Acceptance: Can navigate from a task to a pre-filled compose form.
title: 13.3 — Compose from task context

description: Allow the PM agent daily loop to be triggered from the web UI. Provides a trigger button, live progress feedback via SSE, run history, and date control. Background execution with single-run lock and idempotency guard.
title: Epic 11: Web-Triggered PM Agent Run

description: Refactor the PM agent __main__ block into a reusable run_agent(project_id=None) function that returns a structured result. Captures tool call events as structured log entries. Keep CLI entry point working by calling this function. Acceptance: Agent can be triggered programmatically; events are captured; CLI still works.
title: 11.1 — Extract agent runner function

description: POST /api/agent/run starts background agent run, returns run ID or 409. GET /api/agent/status/{run_id} SSE stream of events. GET /api/agent/runs JSON list of past runs. Run metadata stored in agent_runs table (new ORM model). Concurrency lock; respects existing idempotency guard. Acceptance: Can trigger agent via HTTP; can stream progress; can list past runs; concurrent runs rejected.
title: 11.2 — Agent run API endpoints and SSE

description: Dedicated /agent page with Run Agent button (disabled while running or already run today). Live progress panel showing current step and tool calls via SSE. Scrollable log output. Run history table with start/end time, status, link to journal. Acceptance: Full trigger -> progress -> completion flow works from the browser.
title: 11.3 — Agent run web UI

description: Current REFERENCE_DATE displayed on the agent page. Date picker to set a new date before triggering a run. POST /api/agent/date with {date: YYYY-MM-DD}. Acceptance: Can change the simulated date from the browser; agent runs use the new date.
title: 11.4 — Date control from web UI

acceptance_criteria:
1. Overdue tasks are detected each run and trigger escalation-style messages (not standard reminders).
2. Agent does not chase owners for tasks whose dependencies are incomplete.
3. Tasks have a priority field (high/medium/low) that influences reminder tone and escalation urgency.
4. Owners receive advance warning reminders 1-2 days before due date (configurable lead time).
5. All new fields are in the domain model, ORM, and SQL schema.
6. Existing agent behaviour is preserved for non-overdue, non-blocked tasks.
description: Enhance the PM agent's ability to understand and act on task state: detect overdue tasks, enforce dependency logic, add priority-based triage, and send advance warning reminders before due dates. These capabilities make the agent proactive rather than reactive. Should be implemented after the SQL migration (Epic 2) as all features benefit from queryable structured data.
title: Epic 4: Task Intelligence

acceptance_criteria:
1. Agent identifies tasks where due_date < REFERENCE_DATE and status != complete.
2. Overdue tasks are logged distinctly in the journal (separate section or clear labelling).
3. Reminders for overdue tasks use urgent tone, distinct from standard reminders.
4. Overdue count is reflected in the project health assessment.
5. No false positives — tasks completed on or before due_date are not flagged.
description: Add overdue detection to the PM agent daily loop. On each run, compare task due_date against REFERENCE_DATE. Tasks past due and not complete should be flagged as overdue. The agent should log overdue tasks in the journal, change reminder tone (urgent vs standard), and not treat them as normal pending tasks. This replaces the current behaviour where overdue tasks get the same reminder as upcoming ones. Backlog item #3.
title: Detect and flag overdue tasks each agent run

acceptance_criteria:
1. Agent skips reminders for tasks where any depends_on task is not complete.
2. Blocked-by-dependency tasks are noted in the journal with which task blocks them.
3. Milestone risk assessment considers dependency chain completion.
4. When a dependency completes, the downstream task becomes eligible for reminders on the next run.
description: The depends_on field already exists on Task but the agent doesn't enforce it. Add logic so the agent: (1) does not send reminders for tasks whose upstream dependencies are incomplete, (2) flags these as 'blocked by dependency' in the journal, (3) considers dependency chains when assessing milestone risk. Backlog item #5.
title: Enforce task dependency logic in agent decisions

acceptance_criteria:
1. Task dataclass has priority: Optional[Literal['high', 'medium', 'low']] field.
2. ORM model and SQL table include priority column.
3. Agent tailors reminder urgency based on priority (high = more urgent tone).
4. Journal task review groups or sorts by priority.
5. Existing tasks without priority default to medium.
6. Demo data includes tasks with varied priorities.
description: Add a priority field (high/medium/low) to the Task dataclass, ORM model, and SQL schema. Update the agent to use priority for triage: high-priority overdue tasks get escalated sooner, reminder tone is tailored by priority, and the journal groups tasks by priority. Backlog item #6.
title: Add priority field to Task model and agent triage

acceptance_criteria:
1. Agent sends advance reminders for tasks due within N days (default N=2).
2. Advance reminders are distinct in tone from day-of and overdue reminders.
3. Warning lead time is configurable (via config or constant).
4. No duplicate advance reminders — only sent once per task per warning window.
5. Advance reminders are logged in the journal.
description: Remind task owners 1-2 days before a task is due, not just on the day or after. The warning lead time should be configurable (default: 2 days). The agent should check upcoming due dates each run and send a friendly heads-up to owners whose tasks are approaching deadline. Backlog item #7.
title: Send advance warning reminders before task due dates

description: Conversational chat interface in the web app for project stakeholders to ask questions. Separate from the PM agent — a lighter LLM-powered chat with read-only access to all project data. Uses full context injection (reporter/context.py pattern). Conversation persistence in SQLite.
title: Epic 10: Chat Interface

description: New conversations and chat_messages tables. API endpoints: POST /api/chat/conversations (create), POST /api/chat/conversations/{id}/messages (send message + get response), GET /api/chat/conversations (list), GET /api/chat/conversations/{id} (load history). Acceptance: Can create conversations, send messages, and retrieve history via API.
title: 10.1 — Chat data model and backend

description: System prompt defining the assistant persona (read-only project assistant, not the PM). Context loader that injects current project state (reuse/adapt reporter/context.py). LLM answers questions about tasks, RAID, milestones, journal entries, and reports. Acceptance: Chat LLM gives accurate answers about project data.
title: 10.2 — Chat system prompt and context loading

description: New /chat page with conversation list sidebar and message thread. AlpineJS-driven with streaming response display. Markdown rendering for assistant responses. New conversation and conversation switching. Acceptance: Full chat experience works in the browser.
title: 10.3 — Chat UI

description: SSE (Server-Sent Events) endpoint for streaming LLM responses token-by-token to the UI, rather than waiting for the full response. Acceptance: Chat responses stream to the UI progressively.
title: 10.4 — Streaming responses

acceptance_criteria:
1. Task model has a status field (not_started, in_progress, complete, blocked).
2. Agent has a tool to update task status based on inbox messages and observations.
close_reason: All foundational capabilities delivered in initial build
description: Foundational agent capabilities that were implemented in the initial build: task status tracking and the ability for the agent to update task states. These were prerequisites for all subsequent agent intelligence features.
title: Epic 0: Core Agent Capabilities (Complete)

acceptance_criteria:
1. Task dataclass has status: TaskStatus field with default 'not_started'.
2. TaskStatus is Literal['not_started', 'in_progress', 'complete', 'blocked'].
3. Status is persisted and loaded correctly.
close_reason: Implemented in initial build — TaskStatus Literal type and status field on Task dataclass
description: Add a status field to the Task model (not_started, in_progress, complete, blocked). Without this the agent can never record completion and will keep reminding owners indefinitely. Backlog item #1.
title: Add status field to Task model

acceptance_criteria:
1. update_task_status tool exists with Pydantic schema (task_id, status).
2. Agent can call the tool to change task status.
3. Status change is persisted immediately.
close_reason: Implemented in initial build — update_task_status StructuredTool in tools.py
description: Give the agent a tool to mark tasks as complete, in_progress, or blocked. When an inbox message confirms completion the agent should update the task, not just note it in the journal. Backlog item #2.
title: Add update_task_status tool for the agent
